problem: Can superconductivity be achieved at room temperature — what material structure?
mechanism: Electromagnetic induction in moving biological tissue
hypothesis: Can superconductivity be achieved at room temperature — what material structure? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Electromagnetic. Electromagnetic induction in moving biological tissue directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Spectroscopic analysis with 8 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The important thing is not to stop questioning. — Einstein
